breaking.title: Kolkata warehouse collapse death toll climbs to 11; CM orders audit of building plans
breaking.summary: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari announces compensation of ₹10 lakh to the next of kin of the deceased and ₹1 lakh to the injured; an FIR has been registered and six persons arrested

breaking.title: Venezuela earthquake LIVE: Venezuela quake response requires 'massive collective effort', says UN aid chief
breaking.summary: The powerful back-to-back earthquakes killed at least 164 people and injured at least 971, Venezuela's Acting President Delcy Rodríguez said, as communities across the South American country sustained damage

breaking.title: West Asia war LIVE: Cargo ship reports suspected attack while crossing Hormuz
breaking.summary: On deal with Iran, U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said open for peace without undermining security of U.S., its allies; he is in Bahrain for a Gulf Cooperation Council meeting

breaking.title: Families lay flowers on barbed wire barricade on anniversary of deadly Kenya protests
breaking.summary: Protesters demand justice for the dozens killed during the 2024 demonstrations and last year's anniversary protests.

breaking.title: What we know so far
breaking.summary: The two quakes have killed at least 164 people and injured nearly 1,000, with more feared trapped under rubble.

breaking.title: Kuki-Zo Council admits six Nagas were killed by Kukis
breaking.summary: This is the first known public expression of regret by any Kuki leader or group over the killings; KZC chairman terms it a “great mistake”, says the Naga civilians were killed during an outburst of emotion

breaking.title: Russia denies pressuring Belarus to widen Ukraine conflict; Minsk blames West
breaking.summary: The Wall Street Journal reported ‌that Russia wanted to use Belarus as a springboard to step up ⁠attacks on Ukraine, and that Moscow was threatening to cut financial support if it did not agree

breaking.title: Poland, Ukraine hold key Gdansk conference without Zelenskyy
breaking.summary: Polish PM Donald Tusk called on the West to believe a post-war Ukraine will “rise from the ruins” like his native city Gdansk, largely destroyed during World War II, as he tried to dampen the diplomatic spat with Kyiv
